title: Python DodagState: Parent switch hysteresis logic may need verification
description: In python/src/lichen/rpl/dodag.py lines 156-158, the hysteresis check was refactored from 'if best_cost > current_cost - threshold' to 'improvement = current_cost - best_cost; if improvement < threshold'. Both should be mathematically equivalent (improvement < threshold means current_cost - best_cost < threshold, i.e., best_cost > current_cost - threshold). However, the comment 'switch only if improvement exceeds threshold' suggests the opposite intent. If improvement=56 and threshold=192, improvement < threshold is TRUE, so we DON'T switch and stick with current - which matches RFC 6550. The code appears correct but the comment should say 'stick with current unless improvement exceeds threshold'.
